content:
First note that  
  S = 9 + 9² + ⋯ + 9^{2010}  
is a geometric series with ratio 9.  Hence  
  S = (9^{2011} − 9)/(9 − 1) = (9^{2011} − 9)/8.  

For an odd prime p ≠ 3, p divides S iff  
  (9^{2011} − 9)/8 ≡ 0 mod p  
⇔ 9^{2011} − 9 ≡ 0 mod p  
⇔ 9·(9^{2010} − 1) ≡ 0 mod p.  
Since p≠3, this is equivalent to 9^{2010} ≡ 1 mod p.  

We check primes in order:

2:  S is the sum of 2010 odd terms, so S is even ⇒ 2 divides S.  
3:  9≡0 mod 3 ⇒ every term ≡0 ⇒ 3 divides S.  
5:  9≡4 mod 5 ⇒ 4^{2010}≡(4^2)^{1005}≡1^{1005}=1 ⇒ 5 divides S.  
7:  9≡2 mod 7 ⇒ 2^{2010}≡(2^6)^{335}=1 ⇒ 7 divides S.  
11: 9^{2010}≡1 mod 11 by Fermat ⇒ 11 divides S.  
13: φ(13)=12, 2010≡6 mod 12 ⇒ 9^6≡1 mod 13 ⇒ 13 divides S.  
17: φ(17)=16, 2010≡10 mod 16 ⇒ 9^{2010}≡9^{10}≡13≠1 mod 17 ⇒ 17 does NOT divide S.  

Thus the smallest prime not dividing S is 17.  

Answer: \boxed{17}
